meta.setting: Jefferson wagon road where snowmelt, mud, dry grass, and smoke haze meet across elevation
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in seasonal travel and work pressure

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Seasonal road, mining, logging, and ranch conditions in rugged terrain.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Deer
research_grounding.use: Deer movement and seasonal wildlife sign.

image_prompt.subject: a wagon road climbing from muddy spring runoff toward dry grass and smoke haze, with snow still on the high ridge and deer tracks beside a creek
image_prompt.scene: one grounded high-country road scene, not a split-screen; freight riders pause to judge weather while a creek runs high across ruts, no readable signs
image_prompt.composition: square landscape-road plate with foreground mud and runoff, midground riders and wagon, dry slope beyond, and snowy ridge in distance
image_prompt.camera: third-person road approach angle, low enough to read ruts, tracks, water height, and sky changes
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the season change visible through travel problems, not a decorative calendar scene.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine seasonal road scene
render_traits: slightly simplified riders, wagon, animals, road, creek, grass, and ridge geometry
modest texture resolution on mud, water, snow, grass, canvas, leather, and smoke haze
hard ambient occlusion under wagon wheels, hooves, ruts, creek stones, and brush
mixed cool runoff light and warm dry-slope haze
believable 1899 freight gear and trail clothing
playable route-choice composition where season changes the road
style_modifiers.palette: runoff blue, mud umber, snow white, dry grass ochre, smoke gray, pine green, wagon brown, and pale sky gold.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable signs, labels, maps, calendars, papers, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The road, runoff, mud, dry grass, smoke haze, snow line, wagon, riders, and deer tracks are readable.
Every visible object belongs plausibly to rugged 1899 seasonal travel.
The image supports timing, detour, shortage, wildlife, and weather-pressure scenes.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
